fields.summary: Enable multiple states to to be specified in Resource Manager apps REST call

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

fields.description:
Within the YARN Resource Manager REST API the GET call which returns all Applications can be filtered by a single State query parameter (http://<rm http address:port>/ws/v1/cluster/apps). 

There are 8 possible states (New, Submitted, Accepted, Running, Finishing, Finished, Failed, Killed), if no state parameter is specified all states are returned, however if a sub-set of states is required then multiple REST calls are required (max. of 7).

The proposal is to be able to specify multiple states in a single REST call.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: YARN-1074 added the ability to filter by multiple application states but the YARN webservice does not have the same feature

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A problem which impairs or prevents the functions of the product.
issuetype.name: Bug

status.description: The issue is considered finished, the resolution is correct. Issues which are not closed can be reopened.
status.name: Closed

fields.summary: RMWebServices filtering apps by states uses RMAppState instead of YarnAppilcationState.

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A problem which impairs or prevents the functions of the product.
issuetype.name: Bug

comments.body: In YARN-642, we're addressing a similar issue with the /nodes api.  There we resolved to have an "all" option, but not allow specifying multiple states.  Worth thinking about for this as well.   Although, the number of apps of apps can be much larger than the number of nodes, so being able to have more control over which ones are returned might be more important in this case.
Patch submitted, built against trunk and unit test included.

+1 patch looks good. If there's no objection from more experienced YARN developers - I will commit on Monday.

A couple of comments:
 - You should call the parameter as "states" inline with what we did at YARN-797, YARN-865.
 - Please modify the test to actually have apps in multiple states.

Thanks for the comments, though is it okay to change "state" to "states"? as the following link would need to be changed to reflect this: 
http://hadoop.apache.org/docs/current/hadoop-yarn/hadoop-yarn-site/ResourceManagerRest.html#Cluster_Applications_API

bq. is it okay to change "state" to "states"?

YARN-865 added the filter of applicationTypes. The the query can be composed as "applicationTypes=type1&applicationTypes=type2&applicationTypes=type3", "applicationTypes=type1,type2&applicationTypes=type3" or "applicationTypes=type1,type2,type3".

The patch here seems to only support the first param string. If the follow two are supported, state*s* should make sense.

bq. as the following link would need to be changed to reflect this:
Either way, you need to update ResourceManagerRest.apt.vm to document the new filter of multiple states.
Patch submitted

Thanks for supporting multiple states params. Here're two comments:

1. The states params don't need to be parsed every time when comparing with new iterated RMApp. Do it once before the loop.

2. bq. Are there any examples where an application can be created with a predefined static state?
Maybe you want to schedule some event explicitly, and use waitForState() to wait for a RMApp's reaching a desired state. See the example in TestRMWebServicesApps#testMultipleAppAttempts
Refactored code, changed JUnit test to have 2 apps in different states.

Tx for working on this Trevor.

bq. Thanks for the comments, though is it okay to change "state" to "states"? as the following link would need to be changed to reflect this:
Apologies for saying it this late. I totally missed this JIRA for beta. My comment about renaming state to states was before 2.1.0 beta. Now that beta is out, we don't want any incompatible changes. But I like states better, as it fits in with everything else. (for e.g. applicationTypes). So we need to 'deprecate' the state parameter. So, let's have BOTH - deprecation is perhaps handled via documentation for web-services.

Please add one minor test to validate 'state' along with 'states'.

The patch mostly looks good, few comments though:
 - If user passes in a state that is invalid, then we should throw proper error response.
 - Please add a simple test for the above.
 - Why is the following a container() check instead of an exact match?
{code}
          if (appState.contains(rmapp.getState().toString().toLowerCase())) {
{code}
   Is it for the case of ".. &states=RUNNING&states=ACCEPTED"? If so, please leave a small java comment saying so.

I have added back in the code for "state" along with the original associated unit tests.
A remote exception is thrown when a state param is not in the enum RMAppState.
I also changed .contains to .equals.

Thanks, Trevor.

+1, the patch looks good to me.

Seems like you missed this:
bq. If user passes in a state that is invalid, then we should throw proper error response.
It does throw IllegalArgumentException (and glad you added the test), but I meant throwing BadRequestException like in other cases.

Hi Vinod,

So I will throw a BadRequestException with the IllegalArgumentException as the cause?

I let RMAppState.valueOf throw the IllegalArgumentException automatically, I did it this way because that is how it was done originally and it returns something like this:

RemoteException: {
  exception: "IllegalArgumentException"
  message: "No enum const class org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.rmapp.RMAppState.ggg"
  javaClassName: "java.lang.IllegalArgumentException"
}

An invalid application state now throws a BadRequestException.

I went for the message "Invalid application-state INVALID_test specified. It should be one of [NEW, NEW_SAVING, SUBMITTED, ACCEPTED, RUNNING, FINISHING, FINISHED, FAILED, KILLED]"

comments.body: In YARN-642, we're addressing a similar issue with the /nodes api.  There we resolved to have an "all" option, but not allow specifying multiple states.  Worth thinking about for this as well.   Although, the number of apps of apps can be much larger than the number of nodes, so being able to have more control over which ones are returned might be more important in this case.
Patch submitted, built against trunk and unit test included.

+1 patch looks good. If there's no objection from more experienced YARN developers - I will commit on Monday.

A couple of comments:
 - You should call the parameter as "states" inline with what we did at YARN-797, YARN-865.
 - Please modify the test to actually have apps in multiple states.

Thanks for the comments, though is it okay to change "state" to "states"? as the following link would need to be changed to reflect this: 
http://hadoop.apache.org/docs/current/hadoop-yarn/hadoop-yarn-site/ResourceManagerRest.html#Cluster_Applications_API

bq. is it okay to change "state" to "states"?

YARN-865 added the filter of applicationTypes. The the query can be composed as "applicationTypes=type1&applicationTypes=type2&applicationTypes=type3", "applicationTypes=type1,type2&applicationTypes=type3" or "applicationTypes=type1,type2,type3".

The patch here seems to only support the first param string. If the follow two are supported, state*s* should make sense.

bq. as the following link would need to be changed to reflect this:
Either way, you need to update ResourceManagerRest.apt.vm to document the new filter of multiple states.

Thanks for supporting multiple states params. Here're two comments:

1. The states params don't need to be parsed every time when comparing with new iterated RMApp. Do it once before the loop.

2. bq. Are there any examples where an application can be created with a predefined static state?
Maybe you want to schedule some event explicitly, and use waitForState() to wait for a RMApp's reaching a desired state. See the example in TestRMWebServicesApps#testMultipleAppAttempts
Refactored code, changed JUnit test to have 2 apps in different states.

Tx for working on this Trevor.

bq. Thanks for the comments, though is it okay to change "state" to "states"? as the following link would need to be changed to reflect this:
Apologies for saying it this late. I totally missed this JIRA for beta. My comment about renaming state to states was before 2.1.0 beta. Now that beta is out, we don't want any incompatible changes. But I like states better, as it fits in with everything else. (for e.g. applicationTypes). So we need to 'deprecate' the state parameter. So, let's have BOTH - deprecation is perhaps handled via documentation for web-services.

Please add one minor test to validate 'state' along with 'states'.

The patch mostly looks good, few comments though:
 - If user passes in a state that is invalid, then we should throw proper error response.
 - Please add a simple test for the above.
 - Why is the following a container() check instead of an exact match?
{code}
          if (appState.contains(rmapp.getState().toString().toLowerCase())) {
{code}
   Is it for the case of ".. &states=RUNNING&states=ACCEPTED"? If so, please leave a small java comment saying so.

I have added back in the code for "state" along with the original associated unit tests.
A remote exception is thrown when a state param is not in the enum RMAppState.
I also changed .contains to .equals.

Thanks, Trevor.

+1, the patch looks good to me.

Seems like you missed this:
bq. If user passes in a state that is invalid, then we should throw proper error response.
It does throw IllegalArgumentException (and glad you added the test), but I meant throwing BadRequestException like in other cases.

Hi Vinod,

So I will throw a BadRequestException with the IllegalArgumentException as the cause?

I let RMAppState.valueOf throw the IllegalArgumentException automatically, I did it this way because that is how it was done originally and it returns something like this:

RemoteException: {
  exception: "IllegalArgumentException"
  message: "No enum const class org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server.resourcemanager.rmapp.RMAppState.ggg"
  javaClassName: "java.lang.IllegalArgumentException"
}

An invalid application state now throws a BadRequestException.

I went for the message "Invalid application-state INVALID_test specified. It should be one of [NEW, NEW_SAVING, SUBMITTED, ACCEPTED, RUNNING, FINISHING, FINISHED, FAILED, KILLED]"
